steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a noun showing the action itself
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose ('for/because of') and ِ at end because it's connected to the word قُرَيْشٍ that comes after it

steps.m: to gather or band together (like a tribe)

steps.n: Makes the word into a cute/small version, like adding '-y' in English (e.g., doggy)
Adds ٍ to show this belongs to or is connected to the word إِيلٰفِ (covenant) before it
Changed the word to show making something happen instead of just happening
Added kasra at the end to connect it with the next word
Added 'their' at the end to show who is doing the familiarizing
Added vowels to the root letters to make it mean 'a journey' instead of 'to journey'
Added ة to show this means one specific journey
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of their familiarity/custom (إِۦلٰفِهِمْ)
Connected the root letters with basic vowels
Changed the pattern to make it a noun for the season, with the weak letter و changing to اء
Added the definite article ال with shadda on the ش because it's a sun letter
Changed to 'of the winter' by adding kasra at the end to show it belongs to 'journey'. The 'the' (ال) is written slightly differently but pronounced the same
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
Added the definite article ال and doubled the ص with shadda because it comes after ال
Added و with fatha to mean 'and'

steps.m: and the summer (connected to رِحْلَةَ - journey)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it's connected to the word رِحْلَةَ (journey). Also, the alif in ال changed to a small alif (ٱ) because it comes after و
Adding fatha vowels creates the basic past tense verb form
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added plural marker وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added فَ (fa) to connect this action with what came before, like saying 'and so...'
Added ل (lam) to make it a command/instruction - 'they should' or 'let them'
Added fatha vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Doubling the ب with shadda makes it a noun meaning 'one who nurtures/cares for intensively'
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
Added ه at the beginning to draw attention, like saying 'look at this'
Added alif and fatha marks to complete the word. In this ayah, it's pointing to رَبَّ (Lord) mentioned just before it.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a proper word. The fatha and sukun pattern shows this is a noun.
Added ال to make it definite (specific house)
Added kasra to show this house belongs to رَبَّ (Lord) in the phrase 'Lord of this House'
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to what they're describing

steps.m: The one who/which (describes feeding them and giving them safety)
steps.n: Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it more specific. This word connects to the verbs أَطْعَمَهُم (fed them) and ءَامَنَهُم (gave them safety) that come after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and changing vowels makes it mean 'to cause someone to do the action'

steps.m: he fed them (referring to the people of Quraysh)
steps.n: Adding هُم at the end shows who received the action - 'them' refers to the people of Quraysh who are being reminded of Allah's favor
This is a preposition showing where something comes from or starts
Added shadda for emphasis. In this ayah, it's used twice - first with جُوعٍ (hunger) and then with خَوْفٍ (fear) to show what Allah protected them from.
Added damma to change the verb into a noun that names the feeling of hunger
Added tanween kasra to show this word is being affected by the word مِّن (from) that comes before it in the ayah
This is the basic past tense form of the verb
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding alif after the hamza makes it mean 'to cause someone else to feel safe' instead of just 'to feel safe'
Adding هُم shows who received the safety - 'them'

steps.m: and he made them safe/secure (connected to أَطْعَمَهُم - he fed them)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this action to the previous one (feeding them)
This is the basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (هُم), making the م sound stronger when connected in speech
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to change the verb into a noun. This pattern makes abstract nouns from actions.
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the word مِّنْ (from) that comes before it in the ayah. Together they mean 'from fear'.
